subject: [CCSDK] Make a1policymanagement react on ConfigMap updates
commit_message:
[CCSDK] Make a1policymanagement react on ConfigMap updates

Updated Deployment spec to template ConfigMap files in runtime allowing
live reaction of application to config changes.
Provided Configmaps with scripts to handle monitoring configmap-provided
files.
Updated envsubst to explicitly point to downloaded version.

Issue-ID: CCSDK-2958
Signed-off-by: Konrad Bańka <k.banka@samsung.com>
Change-Id: I22e18f2838c0956f899cb9fa96d9fd862e6c6942

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-2

This cannot be changed in this way.

Init container has to stay where it is.

If you need to modify this file you need to add additional container what will update the file on the volume when CM has changed but the init container has to stay where it is.

BTW
You probably also need to notify the application that its config file has changed no?

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review-2
> 
> This cannot be changed in this way.
> 
> Init container has to stay where it is.
> 
> If you need to modify this file you need to add additional container what will update the file on the volume when CM has changed but the init container has to stay where it is.
> 
> BTW
> You probably also need to notify the application that its config file has changed no?

Well, application is as we've already checked reacting on its own on config file change. I can keep original init container together with current sidecar, but it appeared to me as unnecessary complication if sidecar container is enough to work.

I'm fine to keep original init container coupled with the one I've added (it's as you see pretty same as init container but with continuous polling set up) if you believe it's necessary.

Konrad - I assigned the Jira above to you. Can you please update the image version in values.yaml to version 1.0.1 instead of 1.1.0?  That's the guilin version - version 1.1.0 is built against our master  branch, which could contain changes that missed code freeze and haven't been accepted into guilin.

Info: Patchset 9 (compared to #7) added only correct licensing information + has been rebased on latest master. I think all comments/votes are still applicable ;)

@Krzysztof, @Sylvain - Could you please help me check what's wrong with gating CI results? I can never access onap-k8s results that I see other reviews have accessible (even if they also fail on SO)? Thanks in advance 😊

Patch Set 9: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

> Patch Set 9:
> 
> (1 comment)
> 
> Info: Patchset 9 (compared to #7) added only correct licensing information + has been rebased on latest master. I think all comments/votes are still applicable ;)
> 
> @Krzysztof, @Sylvain - Could you please help me check what's wrong with gating CI results? I can never access onap-k8s results that I see other reviews have accessible (even if they also fail on SO)? Thanks in advance 😊

the test crash when trying to retrieve the version. I'm seeing that our test just check "containers" and not "init_containers", so that's why it wasn't crashing on envsubst...

giving containers without version is a bad habit (not your fault here).
so let's fix that and I'll also fix the status generation script
